rationale: Both candidates miss nearly all the confirmed ground-truth bugs (chunk re-slice rewind bug, ceil/floor length bug, legacy-checkpoint-routes-to-v2-tokenizer bug, WER raw-bytes fallback), so recall is low for both. X investigates more broadly (renames, seq_token_counts, chunk threshold, vendored headers) and surfaces several additional grounded, specific findings (dead ref-codes output, hardcoded sampler params, load_weights fragility), but its confident claim that stream-slot lifecycle is 'handled'/'complete' directly contradicts the PR's confirmed High-severity leak bug (which the author explicitly fixed), a notable false-reassurance error. Y is more conservative and avoids confirmed false claims, but 3 of its 5 'major' findings are repetitive 'add a unit test' filler, giving it less unique signal despite decent grounding. Both give file/line-level actionable suggestions; X's are slightly more varied and specific.
